title: AGENTS.md
text: punycoder R package providing RFC 3492-compliant Punycode/IDN encode/decode functions plus UTS #46 canonical-host normalizer, implemented C++ via Rcpp. Punycode codec optionally links libidn2 present build time; otherwise uses -tree fallback algorithm. Normalization (NFC + UTS #46) always -tree, built vendored Unicode tables pinned one Unicode version (currently 16.0.0). Punycode/IDNA engine pslr rurl packages. file terse, always-loaded working contract. system map (layers, module responsibilities, request lifecycles, build/data pipelines) see ARCHITECTURE.md; behind load-bearing choices see ADR log DECISIONS.md; normative normalization spec see dev/normalization-contract.md.

what: Stale objects: clean-rebuild before you trust a build
title: AGENTS.md
text: Leftover src/*.o files silently corrupt builds two different ways. benchmarking, profiling, header edit, run rm -f src/*.o reinstall. -O0 contamination. devtools::test() devtools::load_all() compile via pkgbuild::compile_dll(), whose debug flags -UNDEBUG -Wall -pedantic -g -O0. unoptimized objects stay src/. later R CMD INSTALL . recompiles sources whose .cpp changed links leftover -O0 objects installed package — measured ~5x slower host_normalize, warning anywhere. Header staleness. R’s generated Makevars tracks .cpp mtimes, header dependencies. Editing header (especially ErrorCode enum src/punycoder_core.h) leaves objects compiled old declarations, producing silent ABI skew rather compile error. Corollary performance work: timing taken without clean rebuild meaningless, timing taken devtools::test() (always runs -O0). Benchmark clean R CMD INSTALL, confirm compile lines actually show flags expect. tests/testthat/test-performance.R deliberately loose smoke check reason — see note top file.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/*.R thin wrapper layer. Exported functions puny_encode, puny_decode, host_normalize, normalization_profile_info, validate_domain, is_punycode, is_idn. validates inputs R (R/helpers.R::.call_with_validation), dispatches *_cpp shim R/RcppExports.R. shims call src/exports.cpp, file talks Rcpp types — everything uses std::string / std::vector lives namespace punycoder. wrapper layer split concern: R/punycoder.R (puny_*/validators surface), R/normalize.R (host_normalize + normalization_profile_info), R/validators.R, R/results.R (S3 print/summary methods punycoder_validation), R/helpers.R (input assertions + validation dispatch), R/zzz.R (.onLoad option defaults). URL surface: former url_encode/url_decode/parse_url helpers best-effort host extraction (never RFC 3986 / WHATWG parser), deprecated 1.2.0 removed following release. New host work goes host_normalize puny_*; URL parsing/canonicalization belongs upstack rurl. puny_encode/puny_decode reject URL-shaped input actionable error pointing rurl::get_host().

title: AGENTS.md
text: declarations live src/punycoder_core.h. Implementations split responsibility — keep new logic matching file rather spreading concerns: punycoder_algorithm.cpp — RFC 3492 reference encoder/decoder (fallback). punycoder_normalize.cpp / .h — host_normalize_one: UTS #46 (non-transitional, STD3) canonical-host pipeline — mapping, label validation, -label check, Punycode encode, DNS length verification. Returns {valid, value} (never throws invalid data; invalid → valid=false, surfaced R NA). Exposes three relaxable flags (check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length); CheckBidi/CheckJoiners always deliberately knobs. punycoder_nfc.cpp / .h — Unicode NFC (canonical decomposition + composition) per UAX #15, used normalizer. unicode_tables_16_0_0.cpp / .h — vendored Unicode 16.0.0 data (combining class, decompositions, composition, UTS #46 mapping/status, combining-mark set, Bidi_Class, Joining_Type). Generated, never hand-edited — see Unicode tables section . punycoder_backend.cpp — Backend selection (select_label_backend) libidn2 adapter guarded #ifdef PUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2. #ifdef PUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 stay file; don’t sprinkle domain code (per CONTRIBUTING.md). punycoder_utf8.cpp — UTF-8 ↔︎ codepoint conversion ASCII helpers. punycoder_domain.cpp — validate_and_parse_domain, label-level rules (length, hyphens, xn– detection). punycoder_service.cpp — PunycodeService facade wires chosen backend domain layer applies strict flag. punycoder_errors.cpp — PunycoderError canonical throw_error(ErrorCode, …) map. R-facing error message prefixes part contract (per CONTRIBUTING.md) — exports.cpp adds prefixes like "Error encoding domain: …". Don’t change strings without bumping tests. exports.cpp — Rcpp boundary: NA handling, strict-vs-non-strict error policy (strict → Rcpp::stop; non-strict → return NA_character_), compare_backends_cpp / backend_info_cpp introspection used tests.

title: AGENTS.md
text: select_label_backend(BackendPreference) returns LabelBackend (pair encode/decode function pointers plus name). automatic resolves "libidn2+fallback" libidn2 compiled (libidn2 tried first, fallback exception), "libidn2" force native, "fallback" force -tree algorithm. Tests tests/testthat/test-backends.R use punycoder:::.compare_backends() (calls compare_backends_cpp) assert two backends agree RFC 3492 vectors (inst/testdata/rfc3492_vectors.csv) representative multi-script domains; tests skip_if libidn2 isn’t available. Note libidn2 path Unix-: configure defines -DPUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 Linux/macOS. src/Makevars.win never sets , Windows builds always use fallback backend regardless installed libraries.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Every public encode/decode function takes strict = getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE). default set R/zzz.R::.onLoad. strict mode C++ layer throws exports.cpp converts Rcpp::stop; non-strict mode failures become NA_character_ per element. puny_encode/puny_decode additionally reject URL-shaped input via looks_like_url_input() callers don’t accidentally pass full URL domain-function. Note host_normalize follow strict/non-strict switch: always reports invalid input NA (never aborts), caller can layer policy. separate contract — UTS #46 compatibility processing, deliberately IDNA2008 / RFC 5891 conformance (accepts labels IDNA2008 rejects, e.g. "☕.example"). pinned profile uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1; normalization_profile_info() returns machine-readable identity must stay sync flag combination passed host_normalize.

title: Architecture
text: puny_encode() (R) validates input character, non-URL-shaped (looks_like_url_input() rejects full URLs actionable error), reads strict. Dispatch .call_with_validation() → puny_encode_cpp shim → exports.cpp. exports.cpp calls PunycodeService, splits labels hands selected LabelBackend RFC 3492 transform (xn-- framing added domain layer). failure: strict → Rcpp::stop contract prefix; non-strict → NA_character_ element. codec Unicode normalization — host_normalize()’s job.

title: Architecture
text: Always -tree, always backend-independent accept/reject output (ADR-003): host_normalize() (R) validates three logical flags passes vector ; never reads punycoder.strict never aborts invalid data. host_normalize_one (C++) runs UTS #46 pipeline per element: terminal-dot capture → map (case fold / map / disallow) → NFC → label split → per-label validation + -label canonical check → Punycode-encode non-ASCII labels → DNS length verification → reassemble. Invalid data → NA element (never throws). Programming errors (wrong type) abort. full normative algorithm worked examples dev/normalization-contract.md.

title: Architecture
text: select_label_backend(BackendPreference) returns LabelBackend (encode/decode function pointers + name): automatic → "libidn2+fallback" libidn2 compiled (libidn2 first, fallback exception); otherwise "fallback". "libidn2" forces native; "fallback" forces -tree algorithm. libidn2 Punycode accelerator , never IDNA engine — normalization always -tree, behavior identical without (ADR-003). libidn2 path Unix-: configure defines -DPUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 Linux/macOS; src/Makevars.win never sets , Windows always uses fallback backend. Parity backends asserted tests/testthat/test-backends.R via punycoder:::.compare_backends() RFC 3492 vectors representative multi-script domains; tests skip_if libidn2 unavailable.

title: Contributing
text: Follow steps order every CRAN release. first three fast-forward ones missed — skipping leaves NEWS.md, published version, main sync. Rename NEWS heading. Change top # punycoder (development version) heading release version (e.g. # punycoder 1.2.0) fold items currently section. news-version CI check enforces top NEWS heading either (development version) DESCRIPTION Version. Set release version DESCRIPTION (drop .9000 dev suffix). Update cran-comments.md submission. Run R CMD build . && R CMD check ---cran punycoder_*.tar.gz clean; confirm platform CI (R-CMD-check, R-hub) green. Submit CRAN. accepted, tag released commit (git tag -vX.Y.Z) push tag. Fast-forward main released/tagged commit default branch always reflects shipped (git merge --ff-vX.Y.Z && git push). Verify: git merge-base vX.Y.Z main equals tag. Create GitHub Release tag. Open post-release PR bumps DESCRIPTION next .9000 dev version adds fresh # punycoder (development version) NEWS heading. title: Design decisions
text: architecture-decision log punycoder: load-bearing choices, made, commit us . entry cross-references authoritative source; decision deep normative spec, log index, spec. Companions: ARCHITECTURE.md (’s built), AGENTS.md (working conventions), dev/normalization-contract.md (full normative normalization spec). Status legend: Accepted (force) · Superseded · Proposed.

what: ADR-001 — Scope: a resolvability- and safety-agnostic IDNA primitive
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. punycoder sits bottom three-package DAG: rurl → {punycoder, pslr}. consumed opinionated upstack packages. Decision. punycoder standards primitive Punycode host normalization nothing . Opinions resolvability, safety, policy never flow . Explicitly scope: spoof / homograph / mixed-script / display-safety detection (UTS #39 / UTR #36); URL canonicalization (RFC 3986 / WHATWG); DNS resolvability, registrability, PSL classification; email/address parsing; per-TLD repertoire / registry-specific IDN tables. Consequences. successful host_normalize() result asserts validity normalization pinned profile — never visual safety. concerns belong upstack (rurl, pslr, policy layer). See README “Non-goals” section dev/normalization-contract.md §1.

what: ADR-002 — Normalization profile: UTS #46 non-transitional STD3, not IDNA2008/2003
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted (ratified 2026-06-14) Context. host normalizer must commit one several incompatible IDNA regimes. IDNA2003 (Nameprep) IDNA2008 / UTS #46 disagree real domains via deviation characters ß, ς, ZWJ/ZWNJ. Decision. Use UTS #46, non-transitional, UseSTD3ASCIIRules = true, CheckHyphens, CheckBidi, CheckJoiners, NFC, VerifyDnsLength . Non-transitional processing matches current browser behavior preserves ß (faß.de → xn--fa-hia.de, fass.de). Consequences. compatibility processing, deliberately IDNA2008 / RFC 5891 conformance — accepts labels IDNA2008 rejects (e.g. ☕.example → xn--53h.example). must always described UTS #46 profile. CheckBidi / CheckJoiners always deliberately knobs; three relaxable flags (check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length) monotone (relaxing turns rejections acceptances). Full normative detail: dev/normalization-contract.md §0.1, §3, §10.

what: ADR-003 — punycoder owns mapping/NFC/validation; libidn2 is a Punycode accelerator only
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted (ratified 2026-06-14) Context. libidn2 full IDNA engine. performed normalization, behavior differ depending whether present build time — violating requirement optional dependency “must change behavior.” Decision. mapping, NFC, label validation, -label canonical check always done -tree vendored Unicode data. libidn2, present, used deterministic RFC 3492 Punycode transform individual label. RFC 3492 output fully determined input code points, substitution change accept/reject output. Consequences. normalization_profile unicode_version properties vendored data, backend; identical without libidn2. Backend parity test invariant (test-backends.R). narrows libidn2’s role “IDNA engine” “Punycode accelerator.” See dev/normalization-contract.md §0.2, §6, ADR-008.

what: ADR-004 — One pinned Unicode version per release; tables generated offline, never downloaded
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. IDNA behavior depends Unicode version. Downloading UCD data build run time non-reproducible break offline/CRAN builds. Decision. Vendor one Unicode version’s tables per release (currently 16.0.0), generated data-raw/generate_unicode_tables.R. Network access happens generation time; generated C++ (src/unicode_tables_16_0_0.{h,cpp}) committed, package downloads nothing build run time. Consequences. Bumping Unicode version deliberate behavior change: edit unicode_version generator, regenerate, commit tables, bump version reported normalization_profile_info(), (per RFC 8753) trigger pslr compatibility review. generated tables never hand-edited. See dev/normalization-contract.md §0.3, §7, §8.

what: ADR-005 — Two error policies: strict/non-strict for the codec; always-NA for normalization
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. Different callers want different failure handling: want hard error, want per-element NA, downstream packages want layer policy. Decision. Two distinct contracts: puny_* / url_* take strict = getOption("punycoder.strict", TRUE) (default set zzz.R::.onLoad). Strict → C++ layer throws exports.cpp converts Rcpp::stop; non-strict → NA_character_ per element. host_normalize() deliberately follow switch. always reports invalid input NA never aborts invalid data (programming errors), caller can layer invalid = c("na","error") policy. Consequences. host_normalize’s contract separate must “unified” codec’s throw--strict behavior. See dev/normalization-contract.md §2 tests/testthat/test-contracts.R.

what: ADR-006 — The URL surface is removed
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted (removal completed; supersedes earlier “deprecated slated removal” decision) Context. url_encode() / url_decode() / parse_url() always best-effort host extraction/rewriting — never RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL parser (percent-coding, scheme validation, robust port/path/query semantics, full IPv6, serialization guarantees). Decision. deprecated 1.2.0 .Deprecated() warning, giving CRAN callers one warning cycle, removed following release: R functions, *_cpp shims, entire punycoder_url.cpp URL parser/host-classifier gone. New host work goes host_normalize() / puny_*; URL parsing/canonicalization belongs upstack rurl. puny_encode/puny_decode still reject URL-shaped input (looks_like_url_input()) actionable error pointing rurl. Consequences. punycoder longer exposes URL surface — pure IDNA/Punycode/host-normalization engine. URL-citations (RFC 3986/3987, WHATWG URL, RFC 5952/4291/6874) left dev/normalization-contract.md surface. See NEWS.md removal entry.

what: ADR-007 — R-facing error message prefixes are part of the public contract
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. Downstream packages tests match error text. Decision. throw_error(ErrorCode, …) map punycoder_errors.cpp prefixes exports.cpp adds (e.g. "Error encoding domain: …") contract. Changing requires bumping tests assert . Consequences. Treat error strings API surface, incidental text. See CONTRIBUTING.md (“Native Code”) AGENTS.md.

what: ADR-008 — Confine #ifdef PUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 to the backend adapter
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. Compile-time backend branching scattered across codebase makes two build configurations diverge hard reason . Decision. #ifdef PUNYCODER_USE_LIBIDN2 guards live punycoder_backend.cpp. Domain, URL, normalization code backend-agnostic see LabelBackend abstraction. Consequences. Windows never defines flag (Makevars.win), Windows builds always use fallback backend — property stays localized one file. See CONTRIBUTING.md ARCHITECTURE.md “Backend selection.”

what: ADR-009 — Single-header declarations; clean rebuild required after header edits
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. core declares everything one header, punycoder_core.h, implementations split concern. R’s package build track header dependencies. Decision. Keep core declarations punycoder_core.h split implementations responsibility matching src/*.cpp. editing header — especially ErrorCode enum — clean rebuild (rm src/*.o devtools::clean_dll()); incremental build silently links stale .o files new header (ABI skew). Consequences. Header edits rebuild hazard, routine change. Keep new logic file owns concern rather spreading .

what: ADR-010 — Trailing FQDN root dot is permitted (documented profile divergence)
title: Design decisions
text: Status: Accepted Context. Strict VerifyDnsLength reject empty root label fully-qualified example.com.. Decision. Capture single terminal root dot processing re-append ; example.com. normalizes example.com.. one documented divergence strict VerifyDnsLength, confirmed UTS #46 conformance corpus (IdnaTestV2.txt, Unicode 16.0.0). Consequences. Leading dots, consecutive dots, multi-terminal dots remain invalid (empty labels → NA). See NEWS.md (1.2.0 Internal) dev/normalization-contract.md §4.

what: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: Converts DNS hostnames canonical comparison form following ratified canonical-host normalization contract: Unicode NFC, case mapping, UTS-46 label mapping validation (non-transitional, UseSTD3ASCIIRules, CheckHyphens, CheckBidi, CheckJoiners), conversion lowercase ASCII -labels, DNS length verification, preserving whether input carried single terminal root dot.

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
code: host_normalize(   x,   check_hyphens = TRUE,   use_std3 = TRUE,   verify_dns_length = TRUE )

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: x Character vector hostnames. NA elements pass NA (missing, invalid). Names preserved. check_hyphens Logical scalar. TRUE (default) UTS #46 CheckHyphens rule rejects "--" 3rd/4th positions leading trailing hyphens. FALSE drops check. use_std3 Logical scalar. TRUE (default) UseSTD3ASCIIRules restricts ASCII letters, digits, hyphen. FALSE admits ASCII (e.g. "_") pinned table marks STD3-disallowed--valid. verify_dns_length Logical scalar. TRUE (default) -label must 1-63 octets whole host <= 253. FALSE drops length limits (empty labels still rejected structural errors).

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: character vector length x. element canonical lowercase ASCII -label host, NA_character_ input NA invalid profile.

title: Normalize hosts to canonical comparison form — host_normalize
text: Unlike puny_encode(), invalid input reported returning NA_character_ (never aborting), caller can layer policy. profile fixed one pinned Unicode version per release; see normalization_profile_info() machine-readable identity. UTS #46 profile, IDNA2008 / RFC 5891 conformance. UTS #46 compatibility processing deliberately differs IDNA2008 — accepts labels IDNA2008 reject (e.g. label whose first character symbol U+2615 HOT BEVERAGE becomes "xn--53h.example"). pipeline draws RFC 3492 (Punycode transform), NFC per UAX #15, RFC 5892 ContextJ rules via CheckJoiners (ZWJ/ZWNJ — full RFC 5892 CONTEXTO checked), RFC 5893 Bidi rule via CheckBidi, STD 3 (RFC 952 + RFC 1123) host-name rules via UseSTD3ASCIIRules. IDNA2003 / Nameprep (RFC 3490/3491/3454) used. default applies full strict UTS #46 profile (uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1). check_hyphens, use_std3, verify_dns_length arguments UTS #46 processing flags can relaxed independently; pass values normalization_profile_info() obtain identity resulting profile. standard UTS #46 parameters, browser mode: CheckBidi CheckJoiners always apply never knobs, full WHATWG host policy (beStrict = false flips exactly three) lives upstack rurl, .

what: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
title: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
text: Provides high-performance functions processing internationalized domain names, split across two tiers.

title: Unicode and Punycode Domain Name Processing — punycoder-package
text: package exposes two distinct surfaces, deliberately kept separate: low-level Punycode codec (puny_encode() / puny_decode()): raw RFC 3492 transform xn-- -label framing (RFC 5890/5891) letter-digit-hyphen checks. performs Unicode normalization. IDNA/UTS-46 host-normalization surface (host_normalize()): Unicode NFC, UTS #46 mapping validation, conversion canonical lowercase ASCII comparison form pinned profile. Use codec need literal ASCII-Compatible Encoding label; use host_normalize() need standards-profiled comparison form host name.

title: punycoder (development version)
text: deprecated URL surface — url_encode(), url_decode(), parse_url() — removed, one release .Deprecated() warning cycle introduced 1.2.0. always best-effort host extraction/rewriting, never RFC 3986 / WHATWG URL parser canonicalizer. Use rurl package URL parsing canonicalization, pass host alone host_normalize() / puny_encode() / puny_decode() host-needs. puny_encode() / puny_decode() continue reject URL-shaped input actionable error pointing rurl::get_host(). internal URL parser (punycoder_url.cpp) punycoder_parsed_url print method gone surface (PUNY-rumdaymk).

title: punycoder (development version)
text: puny_decode() now rejects malformed -label input consistently across backends. -tree fallback decoder previously accepted non letter-digit-hyphen (LDH) characters label’s literal section (e.g. xn--(o)-...) echoed empty Bootstring payload (xn---) back unchanged, libidn2 rejected . now applies documented LDH check decode input reports error strict = TRUE / NA strict = FALSE, two backends agree every input (PUNY-ypjwnagl, PUNY-rxvwqsou). puny_encode(), puny_decode(), validate_domain() now transcode character input UTF-8 dispatching native code, Unicode output explicitly marked UTF-8. Previously host_normalize() transcoded, Latin-1-marked (non-UTF-8 native) host reached UTF-8 decoder ill-formed bytes string produced different answers depending R happened mark (PUNY-egaqhtnc, #67).

title: punycoder (development version)
text: host_normalize() roughly 25-30% faster. Label validation longer re-runs Unicode NFC labels taken straight label split: NFC applied whole host splitting, U+002E safe break point (appears canonical decomposition composition pair), labels already NFC construction. Punycode-decoded -label payloads, never go pass, still checked. Output unchanged every input UTS #46 conformance corpus, supported flag combinations (PUNY-thyalpud).
